verdict_reasoning: B2M is the invariant light chain of MHC class I. It has no TM domain of its own but assembles intracellularly with the MHC-I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C) and is co-trafficked to the plasma membrane as an obligate, stable non-covalent partner. The β2m–heavy-chain–peptide trimer is displayed on the outer leaflet of essentially all nucleated cells, and anti-B2M antibodies bind live cells; B2M is a validated cell-surface therapeutic target (e.g., HLA-restricted antibody and CAR approaches engaging surface B2M). This is the textbook stable_complex_partner case: assembled in ER, trafficked together, surface-resident on intact cells.
